input_row.title: Ensure 'PHP version' is currently supported (if in use)
input_row.remediation: Note: No action is required if PHP is not in use. Remediate from Azure Portal 1. Go to App Services. 2. Click the name of an app. 3. Under Settings, click Configuration. 4. In the General settings pane, for a Stack of PHP, set the Major version and Minor version to a currently supported release. 5. Click Save. 6. Click Continue. 7. Repeat steps 1-6 for each app requiring remediation. Remediate from Azure CLI Run the following command to list supported runtimes: az webapp list-runtimes For each app requiring remediation, run the following command with the appropriate parameters to update the PHP version: az webapp config set --resource-group <resource-group-name> --name <app-name> [--linux-fx-version <php-runtime-version>][--php-version <php-version>] Remediate from PowerShell For each app requiring remediation, run the following command to update the PHP version: Set-AzWebApp -ResourceGroupName <resource-group-name> -Name <app-name> - phpVersion <php-version> Note: Currently, there is no way to update an app's Linux FX Version setting using PowerShell. Default Value: The version is selected during creation. References: 1. https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/app-service/configure-common#general- settings 2. https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/security/benchmark/azure/security-controls-v3- posture-vulnerability-management#pv-7-rapidly-and-automatically-remediate- software-vulnerabilities 3. https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/security/benchmark/azure/security-controls-v3- posture-vulnerability-management#pv-3-establish-secure-configurations-for- compute-resources 4. https://www.php.net/supported-versions.php 5. https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/cli/azure/webapp 6. https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/powershell/module/az.websites/get-azwebapp 7. https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/powershell/module/az.websites/set-azwebapp CIS Controls:

gpt_response:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**PROGRAM NAME:** azure_app_service_php_version_verified

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
The automated program will perform checks to ensure that all Azure App Services using PHP are configured to use currently supported PHP versions. 

1. **API Calls/CLI Commands:**
    - Use the Azure CLI command `az webapp list-runtimes` to get the list of currently supported PHP runtimes.
    - For each App Service, use the CLI command `az webapp config show --resource-group <resource-group-name> --name <app-name>` to retrieve the current PHP version setting.
    - Use a loop to iterate through each App Service in the designated resource groups.

2. **Resources to Enumerate:**
    - All App Services in the specified Azure subscription and resource groups.

3. **Configuration Properties/Settings to Validate:**
    - Validate the `phpVersion` configuration setting against the list of supported PHP versions retrieved from the first step.

4. **Pass/Fail Criteria:**
    - **Pass:** The `phpVersion` setting for each App Service matches one of the supported PHP versions.
    - **Fail:** The `phpVersion` does not match any of the currently supported versions.

5. **Example CLI Command:**
    To check for supported PHP versions:
    ```sh
    az webapp list-runtimes
    ```

    To check the PHP version of a specific app:
    ```sh
    az webapp config show --resource-group <resource-group-name> --name <app-name>
    ```

By implementing this automated program, compliance with the control ensuring the use of a currently supported PHP version can be effectively monitored and validated across Azure App Services.
